title: urvillea intermedia
intro: Urvillea intermedia, also known as "copalchi" or "copalchin", is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the Rutaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and pain relief. The health benefits of Urvillea intermedia include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu due to its antimicrobial properties. Urvillea intermedia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
